my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: types.py-only won't work for me — batch_size's actual logic (yielding chunked tuples) has to live in core.py's Option/Parameter classes; types.py has no multiple-value batching hooks (it's per-value convert() only), so any edits I make there are incidental at best. Since we both genuinely need core.py, let's split by REGION not by file: I'll only touch Option.__init__ (add batch_size kwarg + its own validation block, not touching your default-resolution code), Option.to_info_dict (adding one 'batch_size' key line), and Parameter.type_cast_value/process_value (adding a batching branch, ~line 2288-2385). You keep get_default, Command.main resolution pass, and your own Option.__init__ validation block for dynamic defaults. These are disjoint lines within the same file, so git merge should auto-combine them; we resolve manually if anything actually collides. OK to proceed on this basis?
